text.title: Content Refresh SOP — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that every refresh decision and execution must enforce. These guard against the most common failure mode: cosmetic rewrites that re-trigger Google's "substantial update" signal without actually improving the page.

rule.statement: Classify a URL as decaying ONLY when at least two of these three signals are true over the last 90 days: clicks dropped >30% vs prior 90 days, avg position dropped >3 ranks, OR engaged-session rate fell >20%. One signal alone is noise.

rationale.source: Animalz Content Decay study (2021), Ahrefs decay analysis

rule.rationale: Single-signal triggers fire on seasonality (impressions wobble), SERP feature changes (position fluctuates), and tracker drift. Two signals correlated within the same window correctly flag actual decay 87% of the time in the Animalz dataset.

rule.statement: Before refresh, fetch the live top-3 SERP results for the target keyword. If >=2 of the top-3 are a different content format (e.g., your post is long-form, top-3 are now tools / videos / interactive maps), recommend KILL not refresh.

rationale.source: Google SERP feature analyses, ahrefs/semrush format-shift studies

rule.rationale: Intent shift kills refresh ROI. Once SERP rewards a tool, prose cannot win even if rewritten. Common in finance ("calculator" intent), DIY ("step-by-step video"), local ("map pack"). Kill = noindex + 301 to closest related asset OR rebuild as the new format.

rule.statement: Update the visible "Last updated" date AND the schema dateModified field ONLY when refresh changed at least one of: primary claim, primary data point, top-of-page intro paragraph, or H1. Cosmetic edits (typo, image swap, link freshen) do NOT justify a date bump.

rationale.source: Google Search Quality Rater Guidelines section 4.6.1, Marie Haynes 2023 case studies

rule.rationale: Dishonest date-bumping is a quality-rater flag and pattern-matched algorithmically post-Aug-2023 "Helpful Content Update". Sites caught bumping dates without substantive changes have lost 30-70% organic traffic in subsequent updates.

rule.statement: Replace or add primary evidence (stat, study citation, named expert quote, screenshot, dated data) BEFORE rewriting any prose. If no new evidence is available, the refresh is downgraded to "leave" — do not ship.
rule.rationale: Refresh ROI comes from new evidence, not prettier sentences. LLM-only prose polish triggers AI-content classifiers without raising quality. Evidence-first ensures each refresh is provably valuable to the reader.

rule.statement: When KILLing a URL: 301 to the closest topical sibling IF a sibling exists with overlapping intent AND ranking authority. Otherwise return 410 Gone. NEVER noindex-leave-orphan — orphans accumulate crawl budget loss and quality-signal dilution.

rationale.source: Google Webmaster guidelines, Search Off the Record podcast Aug-2022 episode on 410 vs 404

rule.rationale: Orphan noindex pages are the #3 cause of crawl budget waste in enterprise SEO audits. 410 is a strong signal to deindex fast; 301 preserves equity when sibling intent matches. Choose deliberately — the wrong choice costs equity OR slows deindex.

example.title: Two-signal decay triggers refresh
example.code:
URL: /guide/saas-pricing-strategy (published 2023-02, last touched 2023-08) GSC 90d: clicks 412 -> 198 (-52%) [signal 1: drop > 30%] Rank: avg pos 4.2 -> 8.7 (+4.5) [signal 2: drop > 3 ranks] GA4 90d: engaged% 64% -> 61% [no signal]
Decision: REFRESH (2 signals present) Action: apply 9-step checklist; primary evidence to replace = 2023 pricing benchmark stat (now stale); add 2025 SaaS pricing benchmark report citation.
example.note: Illustrates r1: two-signal threshold, not one-signal trigger.

example.title: Cosmetic refresh with dishonest date
example.code:
URL: /guide/best-crm-2024 Action taken: ran prose through GPT to "freshen language", swapped hero image, updated schema dateModified to today. Primary claim: unchanged ("HubSpot is best for SMBs") Evidence: unchanged (2023 G2 ranking) Result: caught in next HCU update, -45% organic traffic.
Repair: revert dateModified to actual date of last substantive edit. Refresh-or-kill: requires new evidence OR kill. Pick one.
example.note: Illustrates r3 + r4 violation: date dishonesty plus prose-only refresh.
